reasoning: The NASDAQ 100 has been in a steep decline throughout March 2025, falling from over 21,600 in mid-February to 19,281.4 on March 28. Search results indicate this decline is driven by mounting fears of an economic recession, stubborn inflation, consumer sentiment drops, pressure from new tariffs, and a broader sell-off in AI/tech stocks. For the nearest resolution date (April 6), the strong negative momentum and FRED linear model predict a low probability of a rebound above the March 30 base level (combined estimate of 0.31). As the forecast horizon extends from 1 month to 6 months, uncertainty increases about whether this is a short-term correction or the start of a prolonged bear market, pulling probabilities progressively toward 0.5. For the 1-year horizon (March 30, 2026), the historical upward drift of equities provides a slight counterbalance to current macroeconomic headwinds, bringing the estimate slightly above 0.50, but still closely reflecting maximum uncertainty.
